cere: noun. A fleshy, waxy area at the base of the upper beak in certain birds.
cereus: noun. Any of the genus Cereus, either as currently or formerly defined, of plants of the cactus family, natives to the Americas, from
cero: noun. A large scombroid food fish (Scomberomorus regalis) found chiefly in the West Indies.
cerous: adjective. Containing cerium with valence three
ceruse: noun. Synonym of white lead, a hydrate of lead mixed with carbonate, particularly as used to whiten skin or in early medicine.
cor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Cornish.
core: noun. In general usage, an essential part of a thing surrounded by other essential things.
corer: noun. A utensil for removing the core from apples and similar fruit or vegetables.
crore: noun. ten million (10⁷): 10,000,000, that is, with Indian digit grouping, 1,00,00,000.
cruor: noun. The colouring matter of the blood.
cruse: noun. A small jar used to hold liquid, such as oil or water.
cue: noun. The name of the Latin script letter Q / q.
cur: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chhulung.
ecu: noun. The European Currency Unit (symbol ₠), a currency used in the European Community before the euro.
eros: noun. A winged figure of a child representing love or its power.
erose: adjective. Irregularly notched, eaten away, as though bitten.
err: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Erre.
errs: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of err
ers: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ersu.
orc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orma.
ore: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orejón.
ors: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orang Seletar.
recourse: noun. The act of seeking assistance or advice.
recur: verb. Of an event, situation, etc.: to appear or happen again, especially repeatedly.
recurs: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of recur
recuse: verb. To reject or repudiate (an authority, a person, a court judgment, etc.).
ree: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rejang Kayan.
rescore: verb. To score again; to assign new marks to.
rescue: verb. To save from any violence, danger or evil.
rescuer: noun. A person who rescues someone or something.
resource: noun. Something that one uses to achieve an objective, e.g. raw materials or personnel.
rose: noun. A shrub of the genus Rosa, with red, pink, white or yellow flowers.
rouser: noun. Something very exciting or stimulating.
rue: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Carpathian Rusyn.
ruse: noun. A turning or doubling back, especially of animals to get out of the way of hunting dogs.
sec: symbol. Symbol of the trigonometric function secant.
secure: adjective. Free from attack or danger; protected.
securer: adjective. comparative form of secure: more secure
see: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seneca.
seer: noun. One who foretells the future; a clairvoyant, prophet, soothsayer or diviner.
ser: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Serrano.
serer: adjective. comparative form of sere: more sere
sore: adjective. Causing pain or discomfort; painfully sensitive.
sorer: adjective. comparative form of sore: more sore
sou: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Southern Thai.
sure: adjective. Physically secure and certain, non-failing, reliable.
surer: adjective. comparative form of sure: more sure
user: noun. One who uses or makes use of something, a consumer or client or an express or implied licensee (free user) or a trespasser.
